title: dicliptera interrupta
intro: Dicliptera interrupta, also known as the interrupted foxglove or Mexican cigar plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Dicliptera interrupta has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Dicliptera interrupta can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in tinctures and salves.
